program.name: Cerritos Urban Forest
program.description: Since incorporation in 1956, the City of Cerritos has planted more than 30,000 trees. Approximately 250 young trees are planted each year. Cerritos has been named a Tree City USA for 26 consecutive years.

assetValuation.description: Trees are municipal assets with quantifiable financial benefits. The Council of Tree and Landscape Appraisers (CTLA) method and i-Tree tools provide standardized valuation frameworks.
assetValuation.valuationNote: Estimated using average replacement cost method. Actual values vary significantly by species, size, condition, and location.

benefits.name: Air Quality Improvement
benefits.description: Trees remove particulate matter, ozone, SO2, NO2, and CO from the air. Each tree removes approximately 26 lbs of CO2 per year.
benefits.metric: 26 lbs CO2 removed per tree per year

benefits.name: Stormwater Management
benefits.description: Tree canopy intercepts rainfall, reducing runoff and lowering flood risk. This reduces the burden on city storm drain infrastructure.
benefits.metric: 1,000 gallons rainfall intercepted per tree per year

benefits.name: Energy Savings
benefits.description: Strategically placed trees shade buildings and reduce cooling costs in Southern California's warm climate.
benefits.metric: ~50 kWh saved per shade tree per year

benefits.name: Property Value Enhancement
benefits.description: As the City notes, trees can increase property values. Studies show mature street trees add 3-15% to nearby home values.

benefits.name: Heat Island Reduction
benefits.description: Urban canopy reduces surface temperatures by 20-45 degrees F compared to unshaded areas, critical for pedestrian comfort.
benefits.totalMetric: Significant heat island mitigation across city

plannerScenarios.name: Current Program
plannerScenarios.description: Continue planting ~250 trees/year with current maintenance

plannerScenarios.name: Accelerated Planting
plannerScenarios.description: Double planting to 500 trees/year with expanded maintenance

plannerScenarios.name: Canopy Maximization
plannerScenarios.description: Focus on large-canopy species for maximum shade and stormwater benefit

sources.name: City of Cerritos — Tree City USA Announcement
City of Cerritos — Landscape and Trees
